examples.title: Pseudocode: Declare and Initialize 2D Array
examples.description: Declaring and initializing a 2D array with rows and columns.
examples.code:
DECLARE matrix[rows][columns]
FOR i FROM 0 TO rows - 1
 FOR j FROM 0 TO columns - 1
 matrix[i][j] = value
END FOR
END FOR
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates how to declare a 2D array with a specified number of rows and columns, then initialize each element with a value.

examples.title: Python: Declare and Initialize 2D Array
examples.description: Declaring and initializing a 2D array using Python.
examples.code:
rows = 3
 columns = 4
 matrix = [[0 for _ in range(columns)] for _ in range(rows)]
for i in range(rows):
 for j in range(columns):
 matrix[i][j] = i * j
print(matrix)
examples.explanation: This Python code declares a 2D array with 3 rows and 4 columns, initializes each element to 0, then assigns a value to each element based on its row and column indices.

examples.title: C: Declare and Initialize 2D Array
examples.description: Declaring and initializing a 2D array using C.
examples.explanation: This C code declares a 2D array with 3 rows and 4 columns, initializes each element to 0, then assigns a value to each element based on its row and column indices.
